content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected weighted graph it has NNN vertices and MMM edges. 

You are tasked to find the length of the shortest path duvd_{uv}duv​ between each pair of vertices uuu and vvv.

TaskImplement the Floyd-Warshall Algorithm to solve the problem


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a two integers NNN and MMM, denoting the number of vertices and edges.
Next MMM lines describes the edges. i−thi-thi−th of these MMM lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and wiw_iwi​ denoting there exists an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ with weight wiw_iwi​.


Output Format
Output a Matrix of N x N dimension - i−thi-thi−th  line has the distance from node iii to all the other nodes.
If there exists no path from iii to jjj output -1 instead.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M — the number of vertices and edges. Then M lines follow, each containing three integers u, v and w denoting an undirected edge between u and v with weight w.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output N lines. The i‑th line contains N space‑separated integers – the shortest distance from vertex i to every vertex (including itself). If there is no path between i and j, output -1 for that entry.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The graph has vertices 1‑4. Vertex 4 is isolated, so distances to/from it are -1. All other shortest paths are computed via Floyd‑Warshall.
Path 1→3 goes through vertex 2 with total weight 2.
Direct edge gives distance 1 between the two vertices.

solution.approach: The Floyd‑Warshall algorithm computes shortest paths between all pairs of vertices in O(N³) time. Initialise a distance matrix with 0 on the diagonal and INF elsewhere, set the given edges (keeping the minimum weight for multiple edges), then iteratively improve distances via an intermediate vertex k. After the three nested loops, distance[i][j] holds the length of the shortest path or INF if unreachable. Replace INF by -1 while printing. This works for the given constraints (N ≤ 100).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N³) – each test case runs Floyd‑Warshall once.
